trials.supervisor_analysis: Baseline established at score 61.0 (2.61ms optimized vs 2.90ms reference, 1.11x speedup) with thorough profiling showing 78.2% of time in transposed conv GEMM, 9.8% in transpose, 12% in elementwise ops. Advancing to optimization stage.

nudges.text: You still have no baseline score. Consider first creating a simple `ModelNew` that just wraps the PyTorch reference operations (ConvTranspose2d → BatchNorm → Tanh → MaxPool → GroupNorm) without any custom Triton kernels to get a passing correctness check and baseline score. Then you can try `torch.compile` or fused Triton kernels as optimizations on top of a known-working implementation.

You're spending too long debugging a custom Triton kernel without a baseline. Create a `ModelNew` that simply uses `nn.ConvTranspose2d`, `nn.BatchNorm2d`, `torch.tanh`, `nn.MaxPool2d`, and `nn.GroupNorm` in its forward method — identical to the reference — to get a passing correctness check and a baseline score first. Then optimize from there (e.g., `torch.compile` or fused kernels).

You already had a working `torch.compile` solution scoring 61.1. Revert `generated_kernel.py` to that working version before debugging further. The profiling showed 78% of time is in ConvTranspose2d GEMM — fusing the lightweight ops (BN+tanh, GroupNorm) with Triton yields marginal gains. Instead, try `torch.compile(mode="max-autotune")` or experiment with running the model in `torch.float16` / `torch.bfloat16` with autocast for a bigger speedup on the conv.
